req.title: Card PIN change
req.statement: A customer shall change their card PIN at most 5 times per 24-hour window.

doc.reasoning: The requirement enforces a rate limit of at most 5 PIN changes per 24-hour window for a given card. Key test dimensions are: (1) a normal successful PIN change within the limit (EP positive partition), (2) the exact boundary at the 5th attempt succeeding and the 6th failing (BVA), (3) rejection after the limit is exhausted (NEG), (4) state transition from under-limit to at-limit to over-limit (STC), and (5) a decision table covering ownership vs. rate-limit combinations (DT). Tests are isolated via per-test card fixtures that guarantee a clean rate-limit counter, making them idempotent.

tests.name: test_pin_change_within_limit_succeeds
preconditions: Card belongs to the authenticated customer
Zero PIN changes recorded in the current 24-hour window
actions: Issue a single PATCH /cards/{id}/pin with a valid new PIN
expected: Response body (if present) confirms PIN updated
trace: R-FIN-022: card belongs to caller precondition

tests.name: test_pin_change_on_boundary_fifth_attempt_succeeds
preconditions: Card belongs to the authenticated customer
Exactly 4 PIN changes already recorded in the current 24-hour window
actions: Issue 4 prior PATCH /cards/{id}/pin requests to exhaust up to count=4
expected: 5th request returns HTTP 200 or 204 (boundary upper-limit, inclusive)
trace: R-FIN-022: at most 5 times — upper inclusive boundary

tests.name: test_pin_change_sixth_attempt_returns_429
preconditions: Card belongs to the authenticated customer
Exactly 5 PIN changes already recorded in the current 24-hour window
actions: Issue 5 prior PATCH /cards/{id}/pin requests to reach the limit
expected: 6th request returns HTTP 429 Too Many Requests
Response body includes a rate-limit error indicator
trace: R-FIN-022: error pathway — 429 when rate limit exceeded
R-FIN-022: at most 5 times per 24-hour window

tests.name: test_pin_change_state_transitions_under_to_at_to_over_limit
preconditions: Card belongs to the authenticated customer
Zero PIN changes recorded in the current 24-hour window
actions: Transition UNDER_LIMIT -> AT_LIMIT: issue 5 PATCH /cards/{id}/pin requests
Transition AT_LIMIT -> OVER_LIMIT: issue 6th PATCH /cards/{id}/pin request
expected: Requests 1-5 each return HTTP 200 or 204 (UNDER_LIMIT and AT_LIMIT states)
All three states traversed in sequence
trace: R-FIN-022: rate-limit state machine — under, at, and over limit
R-FIN-022: PIN updated and 429 error pathway

tests.name: test_pin_change_unowned_card_forbidden
preconditions: Two distinct authenticated customer sessions exist (owner and non-owner)
Card belongs to owner customer only
Zero PIN changes recorded in the current 24-hour window for non-owner session
actions: Non-owner customer issues PATCH /cards/{id}/pin for the owner's card
expected: HTTP 403 Forbidden or 404 Not Found returned
Rate-limit counter for owner's card unchanged
trace: R-FIN-022: precondition — card belongs to caller
R-FIN-022: decision — ownership=false => deny regardless of rate-limit count
